info.title: BFL API
info.description: Authorize with an API key from your user profile.

get.description: An endpoint for getting generation task result.

schema.title: Id

get.description: Get details about the training parameters and other metadata connected to a specific finetune_id that was created by the user.

schema.title: Finetune Id

get.description: List all finetune_ids created by the user

post.summary: Delete Finetune
post.description: Delete a finetune_id that was created by the user

post.summary: Generate an image with FLUX1.1 [pro]
post.description: Submits an image generation task with FLUX1.1 [pro].

schema.title: Response Flux Pro 1 1 V1 Flux Pro 1 1 Post

post.summary: Generate an image with FLUX.1 [dev]
post.description: Submits an image generation task with FLUX.1 [dev].

schema.title: Response Flux Dev V1 Flux Dev Post

post.summary: Generate an image with FLUX1.1 [pro] ultra mode
post.description: Submits an image generation task with FLUX1.1 [pro] with ultra mode and optional raw mode.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 1 1 Ultra V1 Flux Pro 1 1 Ultra Post

post.summary: Inpaint an image with FLUX.1 Fill [pro] using an input image and mask
post.description: Submits an image generation task with the FLUX.1 Fill [pro] model using an input image and mask. Mask can be applied to alpha channel or submitted as a separate image.

schema.title: Response Fill V1 Flux Pro 1 0 Fill Post

post.summary: Expand an image with FLUX.1 Expand [pro] by adding pixels on any side
post.description: Submits an image expansion task that adds the specified number of pixels to any combination of sides (top, bottom, left, right) while maintaining context.

schema.title: Response Expand V1 Flux Pro 1 0 Expand Post

post.summary: Generate an image with FLUX.1 Fill [pro] finetune using an input image and mask.
post.description: Submits an image generation task with the FLUX.1 Fill [pro] finetune model using an input image and mask. Mask can be applied to alpha channel or submitted as a separate image.

schema.title: Response Flux Pro 1 0 Fill Finetuned V1 Flux Pro 1 0 Fill Finetuned Post

post.summary: Generate an image with FLUX 1.1 [pro] finetune with ultra mode.
post.description: Submits an image generation task with FLUX 1.1 [pro] finetune with ultra mode.

schema.title: Response Generate Bigblue Finetuned V1 Flux Pro 1 1 Ultra Finetuned Post

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [flex]
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with FLUX.2 [flex]. Specialized for typography and text rendering, and for preserving small details. Supports text-to-image and image-to-image editing workflows.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Flex V1 Flux 2 Flex Post

x-mint.content: Best for text and typography in the image. For most use cases, start with FLUX.2 [pro]. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [klein] 9B
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with FLUX.2 [klein] 9B. Sub-second inference with open weights, balancing quality and speed.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Klein 9B V1 Flux 2 Klein 9B Post

x-mint.content: Fastest and most affordable, with open weights. 9B balances quality and speed; 4B is the fastest. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [klein] 9B (preview)
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with the FLUX.2 [klein] 9B preview endpoint, where our latest quality and speed improvements land first. For stable production use, prefer FLUX.2 [klein] 9B (/flux-2-klein-9b).

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Klein 9B Kv V1 Flux 2 Klein 9B Preview Post

x-mint.content: Preview endpoint for FLUX.2 [klein] 9B. For stable production use, start with FLUX.2 [klein] 9B. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [klein] 4B
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with FLUX.2 [klein] 4B. The fastest, most lightweight FLUX.2 model, with open weights that run on consumer GPUs.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Klein 4B V1 Flux 2 Klein 4B Post

x-mint.content: The fastest and most affordable model, with open weights that run on consumer GPUs. 9B trades some speed for higher quality. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [max]
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with FLUX.2 [max]. Highest quality FLUX.2 model for image generation and editing, with the strongest editing consistency and prompt following.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Max V1 Flux 2 Max Post

x-mint.content: Highest quality, for the hardest edits and maximum prompt following. For most use cases, start with FLUX.2 [pro]. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [pro] (preview)
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with the FLUX.2 [pro] preview endpoint, where our latest quality and speed improvements land first. For stable production use, prefer FLUX.2 [pro] (/flux-2-pro).

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Pro Preview V1 Flux 2 Pro Preview Post

x-mint.content: Preview endpoint for FLUX.2 [pro]. For stable production use, start with FLUX.2 [pro]. See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Generate or edit an image with FLUX.2 [pro]
post.description: Submits an image generation or editing task with FLUX.2 [pro]. This is the recommended default model for image editing and generation. Supports text-to-image and image-to-image editing workflows.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux 2 Pro V1 Flux 2 Pro Post

x-mint.content: The recommended default model for most generation and editing. New to FLUX.2? See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Outpaint or extend an image
post.description: Submits an outpainting task. The input image is placed on a (width, height) canvas at the given offset, and the surrounding region is generated by a FLUX outpainting model.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Tools Outpainting V1 V1 Flux Tools Outpainting V1 Post

post.summary: Virtual try-on
post.description: Submits a virtual try-on task. Person and garment images are mapped to the underlying input image slots; prompts steer attribute transfer.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Tools Vto V1 V1 Flux Tools Vto V1 Post

post.summary: Erase an object from an image
post.description: Submits an erase task using an input image and a mask identifying the object or region to remove.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Tools Erase V1 V1 Flux Tools Erase V1 Post

post.summary: Remove blur from an image
post.description: Submits a deblur task. The input image is regenerated by a FLUX blur-removal model to sharpen it while preserving the original scene, objects, composition, and lighting. No prompt needed.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Tools Deblur V1 V1 Flux Tools Deblur V1 Post

post.summary: Edit or create an image with FLUX.1 Kontext [max]
post.description: Submits an image creation task with FLUX.1 Kontext [max]. For image editing, prefer FLUX.2 [pro] (/flux-2-pro) or FLUX.2 [flex] (/flux-2-flex) which are the recommended default models for editing workflows.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Kontext Max V1 Flux Kontext Max Post

x-mint.content: FLUX.1 Kontext [max] is a legacy editing model. For new integrations, use FLUX.2 [pro] (/flux-2-pro). See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

post.summary: Edit or create an image with FLUX.1 Kontext [pro]
post.description: Submits an image creation task with FLUX.1 Kontext [pro]. For image editing, prefer FLUX.2 [pro] (/flux-2-pro) or FLUX.2 [flex] (/flux-2-flex) which are the recommended default models for editing workflows.

schema.title: Response Generate Flux Kontext Pro V1 Flux Kontext Pro Post

x-mint.content: FLUX.1 Kontext [pro] is a legacy editing model. For new integrations, use FLUX.2 [pro] (/flux-2-pro). See [which model to use](/flux_2/flux2_overview#which-model-to-choose).

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Cost in credits for this request

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: Cost in credits for this request

finetune_id.title: Finetune Id
finetune_id.description: Name of the LoRA to delete.

finetune_details.title: Finetune Details
finetune_details.description: Details about the parameters used for finetuning

finetune_id.title: Finetune Id
finetune_id.description: Name of the LoRA to use. For LoRAs from other organizations (public or shared), use the format 'org-id/lora-name'.

finetune_strength.title: Finetune Strength
finetune_strength.description: Strength of the fine-tuned model. 0.0 means no influence, 1.0 means full influence. Allowed values up to 2.0

image.title: Image
image.description: A Base64-encoded string representing the image you wish to modify. Can contain alpha mask if desired.

mask.title: Mask
mask.description: A Base64-encoded string representing a mask for the areas you want to modify in the image. The mask should be the same dimensions as the image and in black and white. Black areas (0%) indicate no modification, while white areas (100%) specify areas for inpainting. Optional if you provide an alpha mask in the original image. Validation: The endpoint verifies that the dimensions of the mask match the original image.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: The description of the changes you want to make. This text guides the inpainting process, allowing you to specify features, styles, or modifications for the masked area.

steps.title: Steps
steps.description: Number of steps for the image generation process

prompt_upsampling.title: Prompt Upsampling
prompt_upsampling.description: Whether to perform upsampling on the prompt. If active, automatically modifies the prompt for more creative generation

guidance.title: Guidance
guidance.description: Guidance strength for the image generation process

output_format.description: Output format for the generated image. Can be 'jpeg', 'png', or 'webp'.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: Tolerance level for input and output moderation. Between 0 and 6, 0 being most strict, 6 being least strict.

webhook_secret.title: Webhook Secret
webhook_secret.description: Optional secret for webhook signature verification

finetune_strength.description: Strength of the fine-tuned model. 0.0 means no influence, 1.0 means full influence. Allowed values up to 2.0

prompt.example: A beautiful landscape with mountains and a lake

seed.title: Seed
seed.description: Optional seed for reproducibility. If not provided, a random seed will be used.

Flux2DeblurInputs.title: Flux2DeblurInputs
Flux2DeblurInputs.description:
Input model for public FLUX.2 deblur.

Deblur takes only an image (no mask, no prompt): the whole image is
regenerated by a fixed Klein 9B KV BF16 blur-removal LoRA with a fixed
prompt, so the caller controls nothing but the input image.

image.title: Image
image.description: Base64-encoded input image or HTTP(S) image URL.

mask.title: Mask
mask.description: Base64-encoded black/white mask or HTTP(S) image URL. White pixels indicate the object to remove; black pixels are preserved. Must have the same dimensions as the input image.

dilate_pixels.title: Dilate Pixels
dilate_pixels.description: Number of pixels to dilate the mask by before removal. Dilation helps cover object edges. Maximum is 25 pixels.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: Tolerance level for input and output moderation. Between 0 and 5, 0 being most strict, 5 being least strict.

webhook_secret.title: Webhook Secret
webhook_secret.description: Optional secret for webhook signature verification

Flux2EraseInputs.description: Input model for public FLUX.2 erase.

input_image.description: Base64 encoded image or URL to use with Kontext.

input_image_2.title: Input Image 2
input_image_2.description: Base64 encoded image or URL to use with Kontext. *Experimental Multiref*

input_image_3.title: Input Image 3
input_image_3.description: Base64 encoded image or URL to use with Kontext. *Experimental Multiref*

input_image_4.title: Input Image 4
input_image_4.description: Base64 encoded image or URL to use with Kontext. *Experimental Multiref*

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the image between 21:9 and 9:21

output_format.description: Output format for the generated image. Can be 'jpeg', 'png', or 'webp'.

webhook_secret.title: Webhook Secret
webhook_secret.description: Optional secret for webhook signature verification

prompt_upsampling.title: Prompt Upsampling
prompt_upsampling.description: Whether to perform upsampling on the prompt. If active, automatically modifies the prompt for more creative generation.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: Tolerance level for input and output moderation. Between 0 and 6, 0 being most strict, 6 being least strict.

input_image.title: Input Image
input_image.description: Base64-encoded input (reference) image or HTTP(S) image URL.

auto_crop.title: Auto Crop
auto_crop.description: If True, crop the input image to the canvas bounds when it extends beyond the edges. If False, an error is raised instead.

safety_tolerance.title: Safety Tolerance
safety_tolerance.description: Tolerance level for input and output moderation. Between 0 and 5, 0 being most strict, 5 being least strict. If you wish to increase the safety_tolerance beyond these levels please reach out to the team.

prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Experimental: optional text guidance for the outpainted region. The model may not strictly follow this prompt; the visual content of the input image is the primary signal. Leave unset for default behavior.

reference_offset_x.title: Reference Offset X
reference_offset_x.description: Left offset (px) of the reference image's top-left corner on the output canvas. Negative values are allowed. None = center horizontally.

reference_offset_y.title: Reference Offset Y
reference_offset_y.description: Top offset (px) of the reference image's top-left corner on the output canvas. Negative values are allowed. None = center vertically.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: Quality/speed trade-off. 'high' (default): highest-fidelity results, recommended whenever fine detail, prompt adherence, or consistency with complex content in the source image matters; slower. 'fast': significantly faster and well-suited for naturally extending most scenes (landscapes, backgrounds, textures, products); may produce lower fidelity in the extended region than 'high'.

image_prompt.title: Image Prompt
image_prompt.description: Optional base64 encoded image to use with Flux Redux.

width.title: Width
width.description: Width of the generated image in pixels. Must be a multiple of 32.

height.title: Height
height.description: Height of the generated image in pixels. Must be a multiple of 32.

output_format.description: Output format for the generated image. Can be 'jpeg', 'png', or 'webp'.

webhook_secret.description: Optional secret for webhook signature verification

finetunes.title: Finetunes
finetunes.description: List of finetunes created by the user

tags.name: Models
tags.description: Generation task endpoints. These endpoints allow you to submit generation tasks.

tags.name: Utility
tags.description: These utility endpoints allow you to check the results of submitted tasks and to manage your finetunes.
